title: 10 best herbal teas for swollen gums

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Great Burnet tea has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria, viruses, and fungi, which can contribute to gum inflammation and swelling by reducing their ability to multiply and cause infection.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The tea contains a variety of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the gums by inhibiting prostaglandins and other inflammatory mediators.

Antioxidant activity: Great Burnet tea is rich in antioxidants, such as quercetin and kaempferol, which can help protect gum tissue from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, thereby reducing inflammation and promoting overall oral health.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sanguisorba officinalis, also known as great burnet, teas helps with swollen gums because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The herb contains compounds like tannins and flavonoids, which have been shown to reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums. The tannins, in particular, have astringent properties that help to contract blood vessels and reduce bleeding. Additionally, Sanguisorba officinalis has antimicrobial properties, which help to combat bacterial infections that can cause swollen gums, promoting a healthy and balanced oral environment.
remedy_constituents: Polyphenols: These compounds, particularly tannins and flavonoids, exhibit anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which help reduce inflammation and shrink swollen gum tissues.

Salicylic acid: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, helping to reduce pain and inflammation in swollen gums, as well as prevent infections that can exacerbate the condition.

Quercetin: As a flavonoid with anti-inflammatory properties, quercetin helps reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums, while also exerting antimicrobial effects to prevent infection and promote healing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used to make teas because of their astringent properties, which help reduce inflammation and ease swollen gums.
Roots: The roots are used to make teas because they contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help soothe swollen gums.
Buds: The buds are used to make teas because they contain tannins that can help reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh leaves and flowers of sanguisorba officinalis in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested plant material in a single layer on paper bags or a drying rack for 1-2 weeks.
Chop the dried sanguisorba officinalis into small pieces using scissors or a sharp knife to release its essential oils.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried sanguisorba officinalis in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release its flavor and medicinal properties.
Strain the tea and drink it immediately while it is still warm to reap its full benefits.

remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: The tea's antibacterial compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help combat bacterial infections that can cause gum inflammation and swelling, reducing the risk of periodontal disease.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea's anti-inflammatory agents, including polyphenols and terpenes, reduce swelling and pain in gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines.

Antioxidant properties: The tea's antioxidants, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, help protect gum tissue from oxidative damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to gum inflammation and disease.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tilia platyphyllos, also known as broad-leaved lime, teas helps with swollen gums because of its anti-inflammatory properties. Additionally, the tea's antibacterial properties help to combat the underlying causes of gum inflammation, promoting a healthy oral environment and reducing the risk of further complications.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant-derived compounds help reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Tannins: The astringent properties of tannins help reduce swelling and bleeding in the gums by constricting blood vessels and promoting the formation of a protective barrier against bacterial infections.

Terpenoids (specifically, tilianthine and tilioside): These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate swollen gums by reducing bacterial growth and inflammation in the gum tissue.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and reduce swelling in the gums.
Flowers: They contain compounds that have a calming effect and can help reduce inflammation, making them suitable for treating swollen gums.
Buds: They are also used for their anti-inflammatory properties and can help reduce swelling and pain associated with swollen gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature leaves and flowers of tilia platyphyllos in late summer or early fall for best flavor.
Clean and dry the harvested plant material in a warm place with good air circulation for 7-10 days.
Use a mortar and pestle to grind the dried plant material into a fine powder with a coffee grinder.
Combine 1 teaspoon of the powdered plant material with 1 cup of boiling water to make a strong tea.
Steep the tea for 5-7 minutes before straining and serving to enjoy its calming effects.

remedy_properties: Antibacterial: Pot marigold tea's antibacterial properties help combat bacterial infections that can cause swollen gums, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

remedy_parts: Flowers: Used for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties to reduce swelling and infection in the gums.
Leaves: Utilized for their ability to soothe and calm inflamed tissues, providing relief from gum swelling.
Roots: Employed for their potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, helping to reduce gum inflammation and infection.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 teaspoons of dried calendula officinalis flowers for every 8 ounces of water.
Steep the dried flowers in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release the medicinal properties.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the flowers.
Store the tea in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before consumption.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea per day to experience its potential health benefits.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and pain associated with inflamed gums, making it an effective natural remedy for gum inflammation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, teas helps with swollen gums because of its anti-inflammatory properties and high concentration of vitamins and minerals. The tea is made by infusing the gel of the aloe plant in hot water, which releases its active compounds. These compounds, including aloin and aloe-emodin, help to reduce swelling and soothe irritated tissues. Regular consumption of aloe vera tea has been shown to promote healthy gum tissue and alleviate symptoms of gum inflammation, making it a popular remedy for swollen gums.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds, particularly aloe-emodin, have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help reduce swelling and prevent infection in swollen gums.

Glycoproteins: Aloe vera glycoproteins, such as acemannan, possess potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which help alleviate pain, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing in swollen gums.
Polysaccharides: Aloe vera polysaccharides, including acemannan, have immunomodulatory effects, which help regulate the body's immune response and reduce inflammation in swollen gums.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, the gel from Aloe vera leaves helps reduce swelling and soothe inflamed gums.
Flowers: Aloe vera flowers have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help combat oral infections and reduce gum inflammation.
Stems: The extracts from Aloe vera stems contain compounds that help reduce swelling and pain in the gums, promoting overall oral health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 to 200 grams of aloe vera leaves from mature plants.
Wash the aloe vera leaves thoroughly with cold water to remove dirt and debris.
Slice the aloe vera leaves into thin strips and soak them in 2 cups of water for 30 minutes.
Strain the aloe vera mixture through a cheesecloth or fine mesh to remove solids and obtain 1 cup of liquid.
Steep the liquid in boiling water for 5 to 10 minutes to create the herbal tea.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh leaves and stems of mentha x piperita plant in the early morning.
Rinse the harvested leaves thoroughly with cold water to remove any dirt or debris.
Chop the leaves finely and add one teaspoon per 8 oz of boiling water for the infusion.
Steep the chopped leaves in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release the essential oils and flavor.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup and serve hot or chilled immediately.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh marigold flowers or dried althaea officinalis root and store them in an airtight container for future use.
Measure one teaspoon of the dried herb or two teaspoons of fresh herb for every 8 ounces of boiling water.
Combine the measured herb with boiling water in a teapot or infuser and let it steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the active compounds to extract.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup and discard the solids before drinking.
Drink the tea three times daily, preferably before meals, for at least two weeks to experience optimal relief from swollen gums.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile teas helps with swollen gums because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the affected area. The apigenin in chamomile binds to receptors in the body, reducing inflammation and pain associated with swollen gums. Additionally, chamomile's antibacterial properties help combat bacterial infections that can cause gum swelling. By drinking chamomile tea, individuals can promote a healthy oral environment, reduce discomfort, and alleviate symptoms of swollen gums, promoting overall mouth health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help reduce swelling and pain in inflamed gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial activity of chamomile tea, particularly against bacteria like Streptococcus mutans, helps prevent the growth of harmful microorganisms that can contribute to gum inflammation and infection.

Antioxidant properties: Chamomile tea's antioxidant compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help neutralize free radicals that can damage oral tissues and exacerbate gum swelling, promoting overall oral health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the gums.
Flowers: The flowers contain apigenin, an antioxidant that helps soothe and calm irritated gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried chamomile flowers and store them in an airtight container for up to six months.
Measure one teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers per eight ounces of boiling water and pour the mixture into a tea infuser.
Allow the mixture to steep for five to seven minutes or until the desired strength is achieved according to taste and individual preference.
Strain the tea into a cup using the tea infuser or cheesecloth and discard the solids, then add honey or lemon to taste if desired.
Drink the tea three times daily, ideally at least 30 minutes after meals, for seven to ten consecutive days to experience optimal relief from swollen gums.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wintergreen teas helps with swollen gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and alleviate pain. The tea's natural salicylic acid content works similarly to aspirin, but without the harsh side effects. Additionally, wintergreen's cooling sensation can help numb the affected area, providing temporary relief from discomfort. As a result, drinking herbal wintergreen teas regularly can promote healthy gums and provide long-term relief from swollen and sore gum issues.
remedy_properties: Methyl Salicylate: This compound has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help reduce swelling and relieve pain in inflamed gum tissue.

Gingerols and Shogaols: These bioactive compounds have natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gums, promoting healing and comfort.
Salicylic Acid: Similar to methyl salicylate, salicylic acid is a potent anti-inflammatory agent that can help reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with swollen gums.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the wintergreen plant contain high levels of methyl salicylate, a natural pain reliever that helps reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort in the gums.

Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are underground stems that store nutrients and provide a concentrated source of beneficial compounds, including salicin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe swollen gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest one tablespoon of dried gaultheria procumbens leaves and flowers for every eight ounces water.
Combine the dried herbs with boiling water in a teapot or infuser for steeping purposes only.
Allow the mixture to simmer for 5-7 minutes before removing from heat for preparation purposes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to eliminate any solids and particles.
Consume the tea immediately after preparing it and drink at least two times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal teas helps with swollen gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and ease discomfort. The tea's active compound, berberine, has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby reducing redness and inflammation in the mouth. Additionally, goldenseal's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial infections that can contribute to gum inflammation, promoting a healthy and balanced oral environment.
remedy_properties: Berberine: This alkaloid compound has potent antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to reduce swelling and alleviate gum pain by combating the underlying infection.
Beta-sitosterol: This plant sterol has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which helps to soothe and calm irritated gums, reducing swelling and promoting healing.

Berberine's ability to inhibit TNF-alpha production: Berberine has been shown to reduce the production of TNF-alpha, a pro-inflammatory protein that contributes to gum inflammation and swelling, helping to alleviate symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the goldenseal plant contain berberine, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that helps to reduce swelling and fight infection in gum tissue.
Rhizomes: Goldenseal rhizomes have been traditionally used to treat various oral infections, including swollen gums, due to their ability to combat bacteria and fungi.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried hydrastis canadensis roots in quantities of one to two teaspoons for each cup of boiling water.
Heat pure filtered water to a rolling boil and pour it over the dried herbs in a tea infuser or heat-resistant cup.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes before straining the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean cup.
Add honey or lemon to taste if desired and drink immediately while still warm or let it cool to room temperature.
Consume this herbal tea twice daily, morning and evening, for at least two weeks to experience optimal benefits.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey teas helps with swollen gums because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and soothe irritated gum tissue. The tea's high concentration of allantoin, a natural compound, promotes healing and regenerates new cells, reducing inflammation and discomfort. Additionally, comfrey's antibacterial properties combat gum disease-causing bacteria, promoting healthy gums and reducing symptoms such as pain and bleeding.
remedy_properties: Allantoin: Allantoin has anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to reduce swelling and pain associated with gum inflammation, allowing for a faster recovery.
Saponins: Saponins have antibacterial properties, which combat the underlying bacterial infection causing swollen gums, promoting healthy gum tissue growth and reducing inflammation.

Vitamins and Minerals: Comfrey tea is rich in vitamins A, C, and K, as well as minerals like calcium, magnesium, and potassium, which support overall oral health by nourishing and strengthening the gums and bones.
remedy_parts: Roots: Comfrey roots contain rosmarinic acid, which helps reduce inflammation and swelling in gum tissue.
Rhyzomes: The underground stem (rhizome) of comfrey is rich in mucilage, a soothing substance that can help ease gum discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried leaves of symphytum officinale in sufficient quantity for the desired dosage.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried herbs or two teaspoons of fresh herbs per cup of boiling water.
Combine the measured herb with boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes to allow for optimal extraction.
Strain the tea leaves using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids before drinking.
Consume the tea warm or at room temperature, ideally three times a day for up to two weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover teas helps with swollen gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The isoflavones present in red clover, specifically formononetin and biochanin A, have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate gum swelling by blocking the production of enzymes that contribute to inflammatory responses. Additionally, red clover's antioxidant properties help combat free radicals that can exacerbate gum inflammation, promoting a healthier oral environment and reducing discomfort associated with swollen gums.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Red clover tea contains isoflavones, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in the gums, making it an effective natural remedy for gingivitis.

Antibacterial properties: The tea's flavonoids and phenolic acids exhibit antibacterial activity against certain bacteria that cause gum infections, such as Streptococcus mutans, helping to prevent further inflammation and damage to the gums.

Antioxidant activity: Red clover tea is rich in antioxidants like quercetin and kaempferol, which can help protect the gums from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy gum tissue and reducing swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used because they contain a high concentration of isoflavones, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the gums.
Flowers: The flowers are used due to their flavonoid content, which has been shown to have antimicrobial effects, helping to combat infection and promote healing in swollen gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh trifolium pratense flowers or use dried flowers in amounts of 1-2 tablespoons.
Combine the flowers with boiling water and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the liquid using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the solids.
Add honey or sugar to taste and store the tea in an airtight container for up to 24 hours.
Consume the tea twice daily, one hour before meals, for a duration of 7-10 days.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to swollen gums that are treated with herbal teas are various inflammatory conditions, including mouth sores, canker sores, and periodontitis. Teas like chamomile, peppermint, and calendula have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease discomfort. Other herbs like sage, eucalyptus, and licorice root also provide relief from gum inflammation and sensitivity. These teas can be consumed hot or cold, depending on individual preferences.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for swollen gums are natural remedies that help alleviate inflammation and discomfort associated with gum swelling. These teas typically contain anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that reduce swelling, fight bacterial infections, and promote healthy gum tissue. Examples of herbal teas that help with swollen gums include peppermint, chamomile, and eucalyptus. Drinking these teas can improve lives by providing relief from painful gums, reducing the need for antibiotics, and promoting overall oral health.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal teas that help with swollen gums is peppermint, chamomile, and calendula. Peppermint tea's antibacterial properties reduce inflammation, while chamomile's anti-inflammatory and soothing effects calm the gums. Calendula, rich in antioxidants, promotes healing and reduces swelling. Together, these teas create a soothing blend that not only reduces pain but also promotes healing. Drinking a combination of these teas, ideally 2-3 times a day, can help alleviate swollen gums and promote oral health.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Journal of Education and Health Promotion, the use of herbal products has been found to be effective and safe alternatives for oral health care. The study, which conducted a systematic review of 49 original research studies, found that herbal product interventions were effective in treating a range of oral diseases, including periodontal and gingival diseases. Specifically, the study found that the use of herbal products such as chamomile and Aloe vera, in the form of gels, mouth rinses, or pastes, may help to alleviate swollen gums and promote overall oral health.
